Title: Development of “Purple Endosperm Rice” by Engineering Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in the Endosperm with a High-Efficiency Transgene Stacking System

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chalcone synthase; type III polyketide synthase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity and health-promoting dietary value.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation in rice endosperm; absence is associated with lighter purple grains and lower anthocyanin content.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsCHS was one of six Coleus structural genes stacked with two maize regulators to reconstruct anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm. 2. PER lines lacking SsCHS together with SsCHI and SsF3'H showed lighter purple grains, indicating these transgenes enhanced anthocyanin accumulation despite activation of endogenous counterparts. 3. Its expression contributed to production of C3G and P3G in endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP) of PER-Z#3 under endosperm-specific promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Endosperm-specific rice promoter (distinct ESP assigned in stacked construct)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; PCR genotyping of incomplete lines; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of multigene stack associated with ~25% reduced grain weight in PER lines relative to ZH11, but gene-specific contribution not isolated.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as part of multigene anthocyanin engineering stacks for cereal endosperm biofortification; should be combined with regulatory genes and downstream structural genes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data analyzed for endogenous rice anthocyanin genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Endogenous OsCHS could be activated, but transgenic SsCHS still enhanced final anthocyanin output.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity and health-promoting dietary value.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in rice endosperm; lines missing SsCHI together with SsCHS and SsF3'H showed lighter purple grains and reduced anthocyanin.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsCHI was included in the six-gene Coleus structural module for anthocyanin pathway completion in endosperm. 2. Loss of SsCHI in partial-transgene lines correlated with reduced purple pigmentation, indicating enhancement beyond endogenous OsCHI activation. 3. Supports C3G and P3G production in engineered endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP) of PER-Z#3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; RT-PCR; PCR detection of gene absence in lighter lines; HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As part of the stack, overall PER lines had reduced grain weight; specific contribution of SsCHI not isolated.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important component of full anthocyanin engineering cassette for cereal endosperm; combine with regulators and downstream structural genes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data for endogenous rice genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper lists accession under ScCHI in GenBank note, but gene discussed as SsCHI.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity and health-promoting dietary value.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to complete anthocyanin biosynthesis because endogenous OsF3H was not activated in endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsF3H was transferred because endogenous OsF3H was not expressed in wild-type or PER endosperm. 2. Its presence helped complete the engineered anthocyanin pathway in rice endosperm. 3. It contributed to C3G and P3G accumulation in PER grains.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High in developing endosperm of PER-Z#3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; RT-PCR showing transgene expression and lack of endogenous OsF3H activation; HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical replacement enzyme when host endosperm lacks endogenous F3H expression; essential in pathway reconstitution.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data for endogenous genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specifically highlighted in Discussion as one of the homologs needed because endogenous counterpart was not activated.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity and health-promoting dietary value.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances cyanidin-derived anthocyanin accumulation; absence with SsCHS and SsCHI correlated with lighter purple grains.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsF3'H was part of the engineered structural module for anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. Partial-transgene lines lacking SsF3'H, together with SsCHS and SsCHI, showed reduced pigmentation, supporting its enhancing role. 3. The engineered product spectrum was dominated by cyanidin-derived C3G and methylated P3G, consistent with F3'H-supported cyanidin branch flux.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High in developing endosperm of PER-Z#3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; RT-PCR; incomplete-line genotype-phenotype comparison; HPLC anthocyanin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to steer engineered anthocyanin profiles toward cyanidin-based products in cereal endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notation varies as SsF30 H due formatting; interpreted as SsF3'H.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity and health-promoting dietary value.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for engineered anthocyanin production because endogenous OsDFR is defective; absence caused white seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDFR is defective in white-grained rice varieties used for transformation, so SsDFR was introduced to complement the pathway. 2. A transgenic line lacking SsDFR failed to produce purple seeds, demonstrating essentiality. 3. SsDFR enabled completion of anthocyanin biosynthesis leading to C3G and P3G accumulation in endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High in developing endosperm of PER-Z#3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; RT-PCR; genotype-phenotype evidence from SsDFR-lacking line H#12; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key required step in this engineered context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority enzyme to complement defective endogenous DFR alleles in white-endosperm cereals; strong candidate for pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly supported by line H#12 lacking SsDFR and showing no purple seeds.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity and health-promoting dietary value.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to complete anthocyanidin formation because endogenous OsANS was not expressed in endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsANS was introduced because endogenous OsANS was not expressed in wild-type and PER endosperm. 2. It completed the late anthocyanin pathway and enabled production of cyanidin-derived anthocyanins in endosperm. 3. Together with upstream enzymes and regulators, it contributed to high C3G and P3G accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High in developing endosperm of PER-Z#3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; RT-PCR showing transgene expression and lack of endogenous OsANS activation; HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential late-pathway enzyme for anthocyanin engineering in tissues lacking endogenous ANS expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion explicitly notes OsANS was not expressed in wild-type or PER endosperm, necessitating SsANS transfer.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Endogenous anthocyanin biosynthetic, decorating, transporter, and upstream phenylalanine pathway genes; OsWD40 (upregulated)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or enhances expression of 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes when combined with ZmPl and endogenous OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator in the engineered endosperm pathway; lines lacking only ZmLc produced white grains.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity and health-promoting dietary value.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for anthocyanin accumulation in rice endosperm; absence resulted in white grains.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmLc was selected as a maize bHLH regulator to activate anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm. 2. Together with ZmPl and endogenous OsWD40, it rebuilt an MBW complex that activated or enhanced 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes. 3. Transgenic lines lacking only ZmLc produced white grains, demonstrating essentiality for engineered anthocyanin production.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP) of PER-Z#3.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; genotype-phenotype correlation in lines lacking ZmLc; HPLC metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms rebuilt MBW complex with ZmPl and endogenous OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overall PER lines showed ~25% lower grain weight than ZH11; specific contribution of ZmLc not isolated.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key regulatory component for activating silent anthocyanin machinery in cereal endosperm; should be paired with MYB and missing structural genes.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data analyzed for endogenous genes; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies ZmLc as indispensable in the engineered context.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Endogenous anthocyanin biosynthetic, decorating, transporter, and upstream phenylalanine pathway genes; OsWD40 (upregulated)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or enhances endogenous anthocyanin-related genes in combination with ZmLc and OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major activator chosen because it has stronger activity than ZmC1 homologs for activating anthocyanin pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant activity and health-promoting dietary value.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in rice endosperm by activating endogenous pathway genes when coexpressed with ZmLc.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPl was selected as the MYB regulator because it has stronger activity than ZmC1. 2. Together with ZmLc and endogenous OsWD40, it rebuilt the MBW complex in rice endosperm. 3. This regulatory module activated or enhanced 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes, enabling accumulation of C3G and P3G.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High in developing endosperm (7 and 14 DAP) of PER-Z#3.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgene stacking; RT-PCR of transgene and endogenous gene activation; HPLC metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms rebuilt MBW complex with ZmLc and endogenous OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overall PER lines had lower grain weight; gene-specific tradeoff not isolated.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong MYB activator for cereal anthocyanin engineering, especially where endogenous anthocyanin genes are silent in target tissue.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data analyzed for endogenous genes; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsC1 was not activated in PER, so ZmPl served as the operative MYB input in engineered endosperm.
